name_zh: 坚忍行动南方
target_or_platform: Invasion force, landing craft, armor, aircraft, unit presence
decoy_form: Physical decoys plus radio and double-agent deception
spectral_features: Optical, radio traffic, human-source narrative
deception_effect: Misled German command about invasion location and timing; reinforced Pas-de-Calais expectation
notes: Canonical example of decoys supporting a broader deception story rather than acting alone.

name_zh: 沙漠风暴欺骗行动
target_or_platform: Coalition main effort, amphibious threat, ground maneuver plan
decoy_form: Operational deception, demonstrations, feints, emissions and posture management
spectral_features: Optical posture, radio/signals, movement pattern
deception_effect: Fixed Iraqi attention toward Kuwait and the coast while coalition ground maneuver developed elsewhere
notes: Useful for studying decoys as part of campaign design.

name_zh: 沙漠风暴飞毛腿搜猎
target_or_platform: Mobile Scud TELs and similar-looking vehicles
decoy_form: Mobility, concealment, possible decoys and look-alike vehicles
spectral_features: Optical, mobility pattern, launcher signature ambiguity
deception_effect: Consumed large ISR, SOF, and air effort; many reported kills were hard to validate
notes: Treat as a targeting-ambiguity case; individual decoy claims require corroboration.

name_zh: 科索沃空袭假目标
actor: Federal Republic of Yugoslavia / Serbian forces
target_or_platform: Tanks, artillery, fielded forces, IADS elements
decoy_form: Camouflage, mockups, decoys, emission discipline, shoot-and-scoot tactics
spectral_features: Optical, thermal cues, radar emission control, pattern-of-life denial
deception_effect: Preserved some forces and complicated NATO BDA and target validation
notes: Strong case for countering airpower with deception plus disciplined emissions.

name_zh: 俄乌战场充气假目标
actor: Ukraine / Russia / Inflatech and other suppliers
target_or_platform: Tanks, artillery, aircraft, HIMARS-like systems, radars, vehicles
decoy_form: Inflatable and physical decoys
spectral_features: Optical, thermal and radar claims vary by vendor/model
deception_effect: Draws reconnaissance and strike attention; supports cost-imposition against precision weapons
notes: Keep vendor performance claims separate from verified battlefield effects.

name_zh: 俄军充气装备与伪装工程分队
name_en: Russian inflatable decoy units in Ukraine
target_or_platform: T-72-like tanks, radars, missile systems, aircraft-like targets
decoy_form: Inflatable and physical decoys
spectral_features: Optical, possible thermal/radar augmentation
deception_effect: Creates false target density and complicates drone-observed BDA
notes: Open-source imagery claims should be treated as provisional unless geolocated and corroborated.

name_zh: 美军演训中的充气目标与假电台
name_en: Inflatable decoys and fake radio signals in U.S. Army exercise
target_or_platform: Artillery or command-post-like target signatures
decoy_form: Inflatable decoys plus faked radio signals
spectral_features: Optical, electromagnetic
deception_effect: Baited opposing-force fires and exposed enemy artillery in exercise context
notes: Useful bridge between doctrine discussion and field experimentation.

name_zh: 俄乌高级假坦克竞赛
target_or_platform: Main battle tanks and armored vehicles
decoy_form: Improved inflatable/physical decoys
spectral_features: Optical, thermal, radar-related claims
deception_effect: Arms race in decoy realism under drone surveillance and precision strike
notes: Good case for tracking adaptation loops between decoy makers and ISR operators.

name_zh: Sea Wolf F-35 充气诱饵
target_or_platform: F-35 aircraft on ground
decoy_form: Inflatable aircraft decoy
spectral_features: Optical, thermal/IR, radar reflector categories, wireless-control claims
deception_effect: Creates false aircraft targets at airfields or dispersed operating sites
notes: Vendor page is primary for product claims; media adds deployment-time and cost context.

name_zh: Temerland 主动豹式坦克假目标
target_or_platform: Leopard tank
decoy_form: Vehicle-based active dummy / mobile decoy
spectral_features: Optical, behavior, possible thermal/mechanical cues
deception_effect: Adds movement and behavior to increase target credibility
notes: Useful active-decoy concept; independent effectiveness remains unverified.

name_zh: 电磁诱饵与假指挥节点
name_en: Electromagnetic decoys and false C2 nodes
target_or_platform: Command posts, formations, radars, C2 nodes
decoy_form: Electromagnetic decoy, false emission, signature management
spectral_features: RF, radio traffic, radar/EMS signatures
deception_effect: Confuses targeting, causes wasted fires, masks real formations, supports feints
notes: Most useful as concept and requirements input rather than a single hardware case.

name_zh: 敏捷战斗运用与机场生存力
name_en: Agile Combat Employment and airbase survivability
target_or_platform: Aircraft, airbases, dispersal sites, sustainment nodes
decoy_form: Dispersal plus passive defense and deception
spectral_features: Optical, thermal, pattern-of-life, force posture
deception_effect: Complicates adversary find-fix-track-target-engage-assess cycle
notes: Operational context for autonomous aircraft decoys and false airbase signatures.

name_zh: INFLATECH 充气军事假目标
target_or_platform: Tanks, launchers, radars, aircraft, trucks, artillery
decoy_form: Inflatable decoys
spectral_features: Optical, thermal and radar claims by product family
deception_effect: Provides scalable false target inventory for training and combat deception
notes: Good taxonomy source for product categories.

name_zh: 可消耗自主系统规模化
target_or_platform: All-domain attritable autonomous systems
decoy_form: Autonomous attritable systems; not necessarily decoys
spectral_features: Autonomy, coordination, distributed behavior
deception_effect: Strategic context for low-cost mass and expendable platforms that can include decoy functions
notes: Use as adjacent concept, not proof of any specific decoy program.

name_zh: 纳卡战争 An-2 诱饵无人机压制防空
target_or_platform: Armenian air defenses (SAMs, radars); decoy imitates a manned strike aircraft
decoy_form: Physical/behavioral decoy (Soviet-era An-2 converted to expendable drone)
spectral_features: Optical, radar return of a large slow aircraft, flight behavior
deception_effect: Baited Armenian air-defense radars and launchers into activating and firing, exposing their positions for Bayraktar TB2 and loitering-munition (Harop) strikes; SEAD by decoy
notes: Flagship modern case of an attritable decoy used for suppression of enemy air defenses; the SEAD effect is widely reported, but individual loss and kill tallies vary by source.

name_zh: 乌克兰假 HIMARS 诱骗俄军巡航导弹
name_en: Ukrainian decoy HIMARS luring Russian cruise missiles
target_or_platform: HIMARS/M270-like launchers, M777 howitzers, Sentinel-type radars; protects real launchers
decoy_form: Physical decoys: early wooden mockups, later higher-fidelity replicas; 250+ mock-ups reported
spectral_features: Optical (UAV/satellite); reported thermal/radar augmentation on later models
deception_effect: Drew repeated Russian Kalibr and Iskander strikes onto worthless targets; cost-imposition on scarce precision missiles; reportedly ~10 Kalibr drawn in the early episode
notes: Specific HIMARS-baiting episode, distinct from the general inflatable-decoy case (MOD-2022-INFLATECH-UKRAINE); strike and expenditure counts are reported, not independently verified.

name_zh: 俄军空中与机场假目标
target_or_platform: Ukrainian air defense and one-way attack drones; painted bomber silhouettes, inert cruise missiles, decoy balloons, inflatable command posts
decoy_form: Mixed: EO/signal decoys, inert munitions as decoys, painted silhouettes, inflatable C2
spectral_features: Optical (painted Tu-95 silhouettes at Engels), EO/radar of balloons, inert missile and drone signatures
deception_effect: Diverts Ukrainian air defense and OWA drones, complicates SAR/EO BDA, and baits expensive air-defense missiles
notes: Several distinct Russian techniques grouped here; painted-bomber and decoy-balloon claims rest largely on satellite imagery and a single analytic article.

name_zh: 以伊直接对攻：真实承诺 I/II
name_en: Iran-Israel direct strikes (True Promise I and II)
actor: Iran vs Israel (with US and allied defense)
target_or_platform: Israeli air bases and air defense; the saturation salvo itself decoys defensive tracking and prioritization
decoy_form: Signal/saturation: mixed drone + cruise + ballistic salvos that decoy defensive tracking and interceptor allocation
spectral_features: Multiple simultaneous radar tracks, mixed threat types, compressed timing
deception_effect: Mixed and large salvos stress radar processing and interceptor prioritization; the April 2024 intercept effort reportedly cost the defender ~US$1B, an unfavorable cost-exchange
notes: Here the 'decoy' is signal-level (saturation and mixed salvos) rather than physical dummies; impact counts (~9 after TP I, ~44-45 after TP II) follow FPRI and vary by source.

name_zh: 2025 年 6 月以伊十二日战争（崛起雄狮 / 真实承诺 III）
name_en: June 2025 Israel-Iran war (Rising Lion / True Promise III)
conflict_context: Twelve-day Israel-Iran war, June 2025; US strikes on Fordow, Natanz, Isfahan
target_or_platform: Israeli air bases and cities; US/Israeli BMD (Arrow, David's Sling, THAAD, SM-3, Patriot)
decoy_form: Signal/saturation plus penetration aids: MRBM saturation, mixed salvos, maneuvering and cluster/submunition warheads
spectral_features: Radar tracks, high-altitude submunition dispersal, maneuvering re-entry vehicles
deception_effect: Saturation and penaids degrade tracking fidelity and deplete high-cost interceptor magazines (Arrow stocks reported heavily drawn down); some leakers reach targets
notes: Covers the US-Israel-Iran triangle. Penaid and cluster-warhead observations are reported or imagery-based; interception and depletion percentages vary widely by source.

name_zh: 胡塞武装红海低成本消耗作战
conflict_context: Red Sea and Gulf of Aden, since Oct-Nov 2023
target_or_platform: Commercial and naval shipping; coalition air and missile defenses; shore-based mobile launchers and decoy systems
decoy_form: Cost-imposition via cheap one-way drones and missiles; ground decoys and mobile launchers complicate counter-targeting
spectral_features: Mixed cruise, ballistic, and OWA-drone signatures; salvo timing
deception_effect: Cheap (~US$2k-40k) munitions force multimillion-dollar interceptor use; mobile launchers and decoys frustrate US/coalition strikes (Prosperity Guardian, Rough Rider)
notes: Core cost-exchange and asymmetry case; per-engagement cost figures are widely reported estimates, and the Houthi ground-decoy claim rests on Pentagon-assessment reporting.

name_zh: 美军 LUCAS 低成本诱饵蜂群（坚定狂怒行动）
name_en: US LUCAS low-cost decoy swarm (Operation Epic Fury)
conflict_context: Reported US operations against Iranian air defenses, 2026
target_or_platform: Iranian air-defense network; massed low-cost decoys to open windows for follow-on strikes
decoy_form: Massed attritable decoy drones (LUCAS) used offensively to saturate and expose defenses
spectral_features: Air-vehicle radar and EO signatures presented in volume
deception_effect: Inverts the Red Sea cost equation: cheap US decoys impose cost on, and create gaps in, Iranian point defenses for follow-on manned aircraft and cruise missiles
notes: Recent, single-outlet, post-2025 reporting; treat as provisional until corroborated. Mirrors the Nagorno-Karabakh SEAD-by-decoy logic at scale, with roles reversed.

name_zh: 贝卡谷地战役（鼹鼠蟋蟀 19 行动）
conflict_context: 1982 Lebanon War; SEAD campaign vs Syrian SAMs
target_or_platform: Syrian SA-2/3/6 batteries and radars; decoys imitate strike aircraft
decoy_form: Signal/physical decoys (Samson glide decoys, the TALD precursor) cued by Mastiff/Scout UAVs
spectral_features: Radar returns mimicking aircraft; UAV-cued ELINT and geolocation
deception_effect: Baited Syrian SAM radars into emitting and launching, exposing them for anti-radiation and artillery fires; about 29 of 30 batteries destroyed; first Western destruction of a Soviet-built SAM network
notes: Historical anchor for decoy-enabled SEAD, predating the Nagorno-Karabakh An-2 case; battery and loss figures vary by source.

name_zh: 箔条与红外诱饵弹（消耗式自卫诱饵）
conflict_context: Air and naval self-protection since WWII
target_or_platform: Radar- and IR-guided missiles; protects aircraft and ships
decoy_form: Expendable signal decoys: chaff (RF) and flares (IR), dispensed by AN/ALE-47 and similar
spectral_features: RF (chaff clutter and false returns); IR (high-temperature, multispectral, kinematic flares)
deception_effect: Break or seduce a missile's lock; modern multispectral/kinematic flares counter imaging-IR seekers, while chaff against pulse-Doppler radar is degraded by Doppler discrimination
notes: Foundational expendables; the chaff-vs-Doppler and flare-vs-imaging-IR arms race is a core counter-decoy thread (branch on counter-decoy).

name_zh: ADM-141 TALD/ITALD 战术空射诱饵
name_en: ADM-141 TALD / ITALD tactical air-launched decoy
conflict_context: SEAD; origin 'Samson' (Bekaa 1982); used in Desert Storm 1991
target_or_platform: Enemy IADS radars; mimics strike-aircraft radar cross-section
decoy_form: Air-launched glide (TALD) and turbojet-powered (ITALD) decoy; chaff, passive (Luneburg lens) and active RF variants
spectral_features: Radar (Luneburg-lens passive, RF active), chaff; programmable flight profile
deception_effect: Saturates and dilutes IADS and baits radars to emit; more than 100 launched in the Desert Storm opening
notes: Lineage: Samson -> TALD -> DARPA MALD -> ADM-160; the historical bridge to MALD. (1950s-60s ADM-20 Quail was the earlier B-52-carried decoy.)

name_zh: ADM-160 MALD/MALD-J 微型空射诱饵
name_en: ADM-160 MALD / MALD-J miniature air-launched decoy
actor: US Air Force / US Navy / Ukraine (Raytheon)
conflict_context: SEAD; combat use reported in the Russo-Ukrainian war
target_or_platform: Enemy early-warning and tracking radars; mimics aircraft RCS
decoy_form: Expendable turbojet decoy (ADM-160B) and jammer/decoy (ADM-160C MALD-J); MALD-X and Navy MALD-N derivatives
spectral_features: Radar RCS emulation; active jamming (MALD-J); networking/swarming (MALD-X)
deception_effect: Dilutes and confuses IADS, provides stand-in jamming, and cues or exposes defenses for the strike package
notes: Active variants ADM-160B (decoy) and ADM-160C (MALD-J). Ukraine has employed MALD; procurement totals and variant status evolve.

name_zh: AN/ALE-50 与 AN/ALE-55 拖曳式诱饵
conflict_context: Aircraft self-protection; ALE-50 combat use over Kosovo and Iraq
target_or_platform: Radar-guided air-to-air and surface-to-air missiles; protects the host aircraft
decoy_form: Towed RF decoy (ALE-50 'Little Buddy') and fiber-optic towed decoy (ALE-55, IDECM with ALQ-214)
spectral_features: RF; ALE-55 repeats and amplifies aircraft-correlated jamming over a fiber-optic link
deception_effect: Seduces the missile lock off-board onto the towed body; ALE-55 covers 1-35 GHz on the B-1B and F/A-18E/F
notes: ALE-50 ~US$32k each, combat-proven; ALE-55 over 3,800 produced and in service.

name_zh: Nulka（MK 53）有源悬停舰载诱饵
name_en: Nulka MK 53 active expendable hovering decoy
target_or_platform: RF-guided anti-ship missiles; protects warships
decoy_form: Rocket-propelled hovering active RF decoy (offboard active expendable)
spectral_features: RF; radiates a large false ship-like return that walks the seeker off the ship
deception_effect: Seduces the ASM seeker to an offboard hovering point; effective 360 degrees and independent of ship maneuver
notes: Australia's most successful defence export; the canonical 'active expendable decoy.'

name_zh: AN/SLQ-25 Nixie 拖曳式鱼雷声诱饵
actor: US Navy and allies (Ultra and others)
target_or_platform: Acoustic-, wake-, and wire-guided torpedoes; protects surface ships
decoy_form: Towed electro-acoustic decoy (TB-14A body plus shipboard signal generator)
spectral_features: Acoustic; emits simulated ship noise (passive) and amplifies and returns active sonar pings
deception_effect: Draws torpedoes away from the ship onto the towed body; SLQ-25B/C/E add active-homing defeat and littoral tow
notes: Acoustic-domain analogue to RF towed decoys; the lightweight SLQ-61 (LWT) is the successor class.

name_zh: 洲际导弹突防诱饵与中段鉴别难题
name_en: ICBM penetration aids and the midcourse discrimination problem
conflict_context: Strategic nuclear penetration vs ballistic missile defense
target_or_platform: Missile-defense radars and interceptors; decoys accompany or disguise the reentry vehicle
decoy_form: Signal/physical penaids: balloon decoys, chaff, replica RVs, IR-signature cooling, anti-simulation, jammers
spectral_features: Radar RCS-matched balloons, chaff clutter, IR-signature masking; anti-simulation makes the RV look like a decoy
deception_effect: Forces the defense to expend interceptors on false targets; the unsolved midcourse discrimination problem (LRDR and NGI aim to address it)
notes: Strategic-scale decoying; directly motivates the counter-decoy/discrimination branch. National penaid specifics are largely classified; cited works are open assessments.
